Skip to main content ☮️ Save the World Poverty & Economic Inequality

Roughly 700 million people live on under $2.15 per day. While global extreme poverty has fallen significantly over the past three decades, inequality within countries is rising.

The gap between the richest and poorest continues to widen, and economic mobility is declining in most developed nations.
700 million

people live on less than $2.15/day
What Actually Works Direct Cash Transfers

GiveDirectly's evidence base shows that giving money directly to the extreme poor is one of the most effective interventions available. Recipients invest in businesses, education, and health.
Universal Basic Services

Guaranteeing access to healthcare, education, and sanitation creates a floor below which no one can fall. Evidence from Nordic countries and Kerala, India shows this works at scale.
Progressive Taxation

Closing tax havens and implementing progressive tax systems redistributes resources from where they accumulate to where they're needed. OECD reforms are making progress.
Microfinance & Small Business

With caveats — evidence is mixed and context matters — small loans and business development programs can unlock entrepreneurship in underserved communities.
Land Reform

Secure property rights for the extreme poor can transform subsistence farmers into productive economic actors. This is politically difficult but transformative.
How to Actually Do It
  1. Donate to GiveWell's top-rated charities for maximum impact
  2. Advocate for OECD tax reform and transparency
  3. Support living wage legislation in your jurisdiction
  4. Push for universal basic services in development aid budgets
  5. Support land rights organizations in developing nations
 What Digital Makers Can Do

You design and build digital things. Here's how those skills apply.

 Individual

Audit your own work for economic accessibility. Do the things you build require expensive devices, fast internet, or paid subscriptions to access? Design for the low end of the device spectrum, not just the latest iPhone.

 Community

Offer pro bono design and development to local mutual aid networks, food banks, and financial literacy programs. Build tools that help community organizations operate more efficiently with fewer resources.

 Country

Build open-source tools for economic transparency — budget trackers, subsidy databases, tax policy calculators. Make complex economic policy legible to the people it affects most.

 World

Contribute to platforms like GiveDirectly and GiveWell that reduce friction between donors and recipients. Build financial inclusion tools — mobile-first banking interfaces, remittance calculators, microfinance platforms — designed for populations that traditional tech ignores.

 Further Reading GiveWell

Rigorous charity evaluation to find the most effective giving opportunities.

 GiveDirectly

Direct cash transfers to people living in extreme poverty.

 World Bank Poverty Data

Comprehensive data on global poverty and development indicators.

 Oxfam

Fighting inequality to end poverty and injustice worldwide.
